svg.text: icalint — catch the invite bug before you send it

g.text: examples/rrule-traps.ics:7: error[T002] TZID 'America/New_York' has no VTIMEZONE definition in
this file; Google and Apple guess IANA names, but RFC 5545 requires the definition and Outlook
examples/rrule-traps.ics:10: error[R004] RRULE has both UNTIL and COUNT; RFC 5545 allows at most
one, and clients disagree about which bound wins
examples/rrule-traps.ics:10: error[R006] UNTIL=20261231T093000 is a local time but DTSTART is
timezone-aware; RFC 5545 requires UTC here (append Z), otherwise the last occurrence shifts per client
examples/rrule-traps.ics:10: warning[R010] DTSTART falls on TU but BYDAY=MO; clients disagree on
whether the start date itself is an occurrence
examples/outlook-export.ics:16: warning[T004] TZID 'China Standard Time' is a Windows/Exchange
display name; clients that resolve zones via the IANA database cannot map it (use TZID=Asia/Shanghai)
$ icalint examples/clean.ics && echo ready to send
